title: Orientation: Type, Value, constructors
analysis: The task adds an Orientation type for representing spatial rotations via unit quaternions. The Type enum (ty.rs) gets a new Orientation(usize) tuple variant where usize is spatial dimension N (2 or 3). The Value enum (value.rs) gets a new Orientation { w, x, y, z } struct variant storing the quaternion components as f64. Five stdlib constructor functions are added to eval_builtin() in lib.rs. All constructors normalize the quaternion to unit length and return Undef for invalid inputs (NaN, Inf, zero-length). The Type enum derives Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq/Hash so those are automatic; Display is manual and needs a new arm. The Value enum has manual impls for PartialEq, Ord, Display, and content_hash — all need new arms. Two downstream crates (reify-lsp, reify-constraints) have exhaustive match statements on Value that will fail to compile without an Orientation arm.

prerequisites.description: Verify current workspace compiles and tests pass on the worktree baseline (cargo check --workspace)

steps.description: Write failing tests for Type::Orientation in ty.rs: variant construction with Orientation(3), Display format 'Orientation3', factory method Type::orientation(n), Eq/Hash consistency, is_numeric() returns false, as_name() returns None
Add Orientation(usize) variant to Type enum, add orientation(n) factory method, add Display arm writing 'Orientation{n}'. Derives (Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash) are automatic.

Write failing tests for Value::Orientation in value.rs: variant construction, PartialEq (same quaternion equal, different not equal, NaN bitwise equality via to_bits), Ord (type_tag ordering, within-type lexicographic on w/x/y/z bits), Display format '[w, x, y, z]q', content_hash (tag=16, NaN canonicalization, distinct from other variants)

Add Orientation { w: f64, x: f64, y: f64, z: f64 } variant to Value enum. Add arms to: content_hash (tag=16 with NaN canonicalization for all 4 components), PartialEq (bitwise f64 comparison), Ord (type_tag=15, compare w/x/y/z via to_bits), Display (format as quaternion). Add is_undef/as_f64/dimension pass-through (Orientation is not numeric, dimension is DIMENSIONLESS).

Fix downstream exhaustive matches: add Value::Orientation arm to format_value() in reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs (format as quaternion string), add Value::Orientation arm to domain classifier in reify-constraints/src/classifier.rs (Orientation doesn't contribute to domain classification, same as Complex/Tensor). Add Type::Orientation and Value::Orientation to exports.rs verification test.
Write failing test for orient_identity: no-arg call returns Value::Orientation { w: 1.0, x: 0.0, y: 0.0, z: 0.0 }; call with args returns Undef
Add orient_identity match arm in eval_builtin: check args.is_empty(), return identity quaternion (1,0,0,0)

Write failing tests for orient_quaternion(w,x,y,z): (1) unnormalized input (2,0,0,0) normalizes to (1,0,0,0); (2) already normalized input preserved; (3) arbitrary input normalizes correctly; (4) zero quaternion (0,0,0,0) returns Undef; (5) NaN/Inf input returns Undef; (6) wrong arg count returns Undef

Add orient_quaternion match arm in eval_builtin: extract 4 f64 args, compute norm, reject zero/non-finite, divide by norm, return normalized Orientation. Add normalize_quaternion(w,x,y,z) -> Option<Value> helper function.

Write failing tests for orient_axis_angle(axis, angle): (1) 90-deg rotation around Z axis produces correct quaternion (w=cos(pi/4), x=0, y=0, z=sin(pi/4)); (2) 180-deg around X axis; (3) angle as Angle Scalar accepted; (4) zero-length axis returns Undef; (5) non-3D axis returns Undef; (6) wrong arg count returns Undef

Add orient_axis_angle match arm: extract axis via tensor_components_f64 (must be 3 components), extract angle via trig_input pattern (accept Real or Angle Scalar), normalize axis, compute q = (cos(theta/2), sin(theta/2)*axis), return via normalize_quaternion

Write failing tests for orient_euler(convention, a, b, c): (1) 'xyz' intrinsic with known angles produces correct quaternion; (2) 'zyx' convention; (3) all-zero angles produce identity; (4) invalid convention string returns Undef; (5) non-string convention returns Undef; (6) angles as Angle Scalar accepted; (7) wrong arg count returns Undef

Add orient_euler match arm: extract convention as String, extract 3 angles via trig_input pattern, implement intrinsic Euler-to-quaternion conversion for 6 Tait-Bryan conventions (xyz, xzy, yxz, yzx, zxy, zyx) and 6 proper Euler conventions (xyx, xzx, yxy, yzy, zxz, zyz) by composing elementary rotation quaternions. Return Undef for unrecognized conventions.

Write failing tests for orient_basis(x_axis, y_axis, z_axis): (1) identity basis vectors produce identity quaternion; (2) 90-deg rotated basis produces correct quaternion; (3) non-orthogonal basis returns Undef; (4) non-3D vectors return Undef; (5) zero-length vector returns Undef; (6) wrong arg count returns Undef

Add orient_basis match arm: extract 3 axis vectors via tensor_components_f64 (each must be 3 components), verify approximate orthonormality (dot products near zero, magnitudes near 1, within tolerance), build rotation matrix from basis vectors, convert rotation matrix to quaternion using Shepperd's method, return via normalize_quaternion. Return Undef for non-orthonormal input.

Review fix [untested_core_behavior]: Add orient_euler compound rotation tests that exercise quat_mul with multiple non-identity operands. (1) orient_euler('xyz', π/2, π/2, 0) — two non-zero angles — verifies q_x(π/2)*q_y(π/2) composition yields (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5); (2) orient_euler('zyx', π/3, π/4, π/6) — three non-zero angles — verifies full three-way quat_mul composition against analytically computed result; (3) orient_euler('xzx', π/2, π/2, 0) — proper Euler convention with compound rotation. These tests validate the quat_mul Hamilton product and composition ordering that single-axis tests cannot catch.

Review fix [missing_validation]: Add orient_basis left-handed (improper rotation) rejection tests. (1) x=(1,0,0), y=(0,1,0), z=(0,0,-1) — reflection through XY plane, det=-1 — must return Undef; (2) x=(1,0,0), y=(0,0,1), z=(0,1,0) — another left-handed basis, det=-1 — must return Undef; (3) verify that a valid right-handed basis near tolerance still passes (sanity check). These tests will FAIL against the current implementation because orient_basis only checks orthonormality but not handedness (determinant).

Review fix [missing_validation]: Add handedness validation to orient_basis. After the dot-product orthonormality checks (line ~448), compute the scalar triple product (determinant): det = xc[0]*(yc[1]*zc[2] - yc[2]*zc[1]) + xc[1]*(yc[2]*zc[0] - yc[0]*zc[2]) + xc[2]*(yc[0]*zc[1] - yc[1]*zc[0]). Return Value::Undef if (det - 1.0).abs() > tol. This rejects left-handed orthonormal bases (det=-1), reflections, and improper rotations that pass orthonormality checks but are not in SO(3).

design_decisions.decision: Type::Orientation(usize) as a tuple variant, not struct variant
design_decisions.rationale: Only one parameter (spatial dimension N), matching task spec. Simpler than struct variant like Point { n, quantity } since orientations are inherently dimensionless — no quantity parameter needed.

design_decisions.decision: Value::Orientation { w, x, y, z } stores raw quaternion components as f64
design_decisions.rationale: Matches existing patterns like Value::Complex { re, im, dimension }. Quaternion components are always dimensionless so no DimensionVector needed. f64 provides sufficient precision for rotation representation.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tag=16 for Orientation, Ord type_tag=15
design_decisions.rationale: Next available slots after Complex (content_hash tag=15, Ord type_tag=14). Tags 0-15 are used by existing variants plus the reserved Satisfaction tag at position 10.

design_decisions.decision: All constructors normalize to unit quaternion unconditionally
design_decisions.rationale: Unit quaternion invariant simplifies downstream rotation composition, interpolation, and conversion. Normalization is cheap (one sqrt + 4 divides) and prevents drift. Follows the 'correct by construction' pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Return Value::Undef for invalid inputs (NaN, Inf, zero-length) rather than panicking
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with existing stdlib pattern — all functions return Undef for type mismatches and domain errors. Follows 'silent defaults should be noisy' convention (Undef propagates visibly rather than silently producing garbage).

design_decisions.decision: orient_euler supports 12 conventions (6 Tait-Bryan + 6 proper Euler) via string parameter
design_decisions.rationale: Covers all standard rotation conventions used in engineering (aerospace, robotics, CAD). String parameter ('xyz', 'zyx', etc.) is self-documenting. Invalid convention returns Undef. Implemented by composing elementary quaternion rotations — no external dependency needed.

design_decisions.decision: orient_basis validates approximate orthonormality with tolerance
design_decisions.rationale: Exact orthonormality is unrealistic with floating-point inputs. A tolerance (e.g., 1e-6) allows for minor numerical drift while rejecting clearly non-orthogonal bases. Returns Undef for bases outside tolerance to keep errors noisy.

design_decisions.decision: Downstream crate fixes (reify-lsp, reify-constraints) included in task scope
design_decisions.rationale: Both crates have exhaustive match statements on Value that will fail to compile when Orientation is added. Fixes are trivial (one match arm each) but required for workspace-wide cargo check/test to pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use Shepperd's method for rotation-matrix-to-quaternion conversion in orient_basis
design_decisions.rationale: Shepperd's method is numerically stable for all rotation matrices (avoids the singularity at trace=-1 that affects naive methods). Well-established algorithm used in production rotation libraries.

design_decisions.decision: orient_basis validates handedness via scalar triple product (determinant)
design_decisions.rationale: Review fix: orthonormality alone is necessary but not sufficient for a valid rotation basis. A left-handed orthonormal basis (det=-1) passes unit-magnitude and perpendicularity checks but represents an improper rotation (reflection), not a member of SO(3). Shepperd's method silently produces a geometrically meaningless quaternion for such inputs. The determinant check (det ≈ +1.0 within tolerance) ensures only proper rotations are accepted; improper inputs return Undef to keep errors noisy.

reuse.how: Extract axis vector components in orient_axis_angle and orient_basis. Validates tensor structure, extracts f64 values, and checks dimension consistency.
Extract angle value in radians from orient_axis_angle and orient_euler arguments. Accepts both bare Real and Angle Scalar, rejects non-angle dimensioned Scalars.
Model the normalize_quaternion helper after this — return None/Undef for non-finite results.
Follow the same arity-checking closure pattern for orient_axis_angle (binary), orient_euler (4-arg custom), orient_quaternion (4-arg custom).
Create analogous assert_orientation_approx! macro for testing quaternion values with floating-point tolerance (1e-12).
Follow same pattern for Orientation in content_hash (NaN canonicalization on each f64 component), PartialEq (bitwise f64 comparison), Ord (compare component bits), Display (formatted output).
Add Orientation arm following the same formatting convention as Complex — display quaternion components with appropriate formatting.
Add Orientation to the same multi-pattern arm as Complex/Tensor/Lambda/Field — orientations don't contribute to domain classification.
Reuse the same tolerance for the determinant check — (det - 1.0).abs() > tol. Consistent with existing dot-product and magnitude tolerance.
